drivetrain.reasoning: R1: Swerve mandatory for 2022+. Speed-geared — fast cycles between terminal and hub are the primary differentiator (R11). MK4i L2 for balance of speed and reliability. 27x27 square frame for symmetrical shooting angles.

intake.reasoning: R2: Full-width intake for maximum capture zone. R3: Flex wheels for rubber/foam balls — good grip without over-compression. Over-bumper with deploy pivot to reach ground cargo. R17: Intake on front, shooter on back — cargo flows through conveyor.

scorer.reasoning: R4: Upper hub at 8'2" — ranged flywheel shooter is the clear choice. R6: Hub is FIXED location at field center, not distributed — turret is optional per the 4-quadrant matrix, but skipping saves 3-5 lbs and 20+ dev hours. Aim by rotating drivetrain (swerve makes this fast). R19: Upper hub is UNCAPPED at 2 pts/cargo teleop — this is THE differentiator. Lower hub only 1 pt — not worth optimizing for.

endgame.reasoning: R7: Climb is worth up to 15 pts (traversal) = ~25% of a strong alliance score — MUST build climber. Traversal at 6'6" requires ~60" of reach from frame top. Telescoping arms to reach high bar then swing to traversal. R7: Target <2 seconds fully hung. Two NEO motors with high reduction (60:1+) for holding torque.

autonomous.reasoning: R8: 3-piece auto is championship baseline. Score preload in upper hub (4 pts auto), pick up 2 nearby floor cargo and shoot both (8 pts auto), taxi for 5 pts. Total: 17 pts auto. R11: Optimize cycle speed — 3.5s target per cycle in auto.

scoring_analysis.primary_method: Upper hub throughput (UNCAPPED, 2 pts teleop / 4 pts auto)
scoring_analysis.reasoning: R19: Both hubs are UNCAPPED — no saturation ceiling. Upper hub at 2x lower hub value is the clear primary. 3 good robots will NOT saturate scoring — more cargo always = more points. Cargo RP at 20 cargo = ~7 per robot achievable. Climb RP at 16 pts = needs 2 mid-or-higher climbs. Traversal (15 pts) is extremely high value and differentiates at playoffs.
